english: glowing embers, live or burning coals, mdág-mai doṅ (mdag ma'i dong) a pit for keeping them, e.g. for the purpose of melting metals Stg.

also mdaṅs (mdangs), 1. C., B. yesterday evening, last night, frq.; mdaṅ-gi rmi-lam (mdang gi rmi lam), also mdaṅ-sum-gyi rmi-lam (mdang sum gyi rmi lam) Glr., Pth., last night's dream. — 2. W. yesterday (cf ǩa-rtsáṅ (kha rtsang)); mdaṅ-sáṅ (mdang sang) Lex., Cs.: 'yesterday and to-morrow, now-a-days'; perh. erron. for deṅ-sáṅ (deng sang).
Sch.: mdaṅ-bai ynas (mdang ba'i gnas) place of cremation, the spot where the burning of the dead takes place.

I. Ssk. ओजस्, तेजस्, 1. resp. sku mdaṅs (sku mdangs) brightness of face, fresh and healthy complexion, also with bźin-gyi (bzhin gyi) Cs.; mig-gi mdaṅs (mig gi mdangs) bright eyes Lt.; gzi-mdaṅs (gzi mdangs) = mdaṅs (mdangs); dmár-mdaṅs (dmar mdangs) fresh, ruddy complexion Glr.; dmár-bai mdaṅs-kyis (dmar ba'i mdangs kyis) with a face beaming with joy Dzl. and elsewh.; the brightness is destroyed by disease, འཕྲོག་ °prog ('phrog), frq., or is fading away, འཆོར་ °čor ('chor) Lt.; in a relative sense: appearance, exterior, look, mdaṅs-ṅán (mdangs ngan) bad, ugly appearance S.g. — 2. Med.: a hypothetical fluid, the most subtile part of the semen, a substance that pervades the whole body, esp. the skin, and is the primary source of vitality; cf. Wise, Hindu Syst. of Med., Calcutta 1845, p. 42. 54. 201. — mdaṅs-bsgyúr (mdangs bsgyur) n. of a species of bile. — 3. brightness, lustre, splendour, in general, nyi-mai (nyi ma'i), zlai (zla'i) B. and col.; fig.: dbaṅ-poi mdaṅs-ma (dbang po'i mdangs ma) mig ni ṅá-la med (mig ni nga la med) Pth. I am destitute of the eye, that brightest of the senses, as much as: the most excellent of possessions is denied to me. II. resp. dprál-ba (dpral ba) forehead.

1. arrow, rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa), འཕེན་པ་ °pén-pa ('phen pa) to shoot (an arrow); smyúg-mda (smyug mda') an arrow of reed, lcágs-mda (lcags mda') an iron arrow; dúg-mda (dug mda') a poisoned arrow Mil.; dprál-bai mda (dpral ba'i mda') an arrow lodged in the forehead Glr.; mé-mda (me mda') 1. a fiery dart. 2. gun, fire-lock C. — 2. any straight and thin pole or piece of wood, e.g. the stem or tube of a tobacco-pipe; śiṅ-rtai mda (shing rta'i mda') pole or beam of a carriage; lcágs-mda (lcags mda') an iron bar or rod, a ramrod etc.; čú-mda (chu mda') a jet or shoot of water, frq.; *(s)kár-da* (*skar mda'*) W. a shooting star. — 3. = mdo (mdo) 1. — 4. symb. num.: 5. Comp. mda-kúṅ (mda' khung) loop-hole, embrasure. — mdá-mǩan (mda' mkhan) 1. an archer. 2. an arrow-maker Glr. — mda-rgyáṅ (mda' rgyang) the range of an arrow-shot Glr. — mda-sgró (mda' sgro) the feathers of an arrow Cs. — mdá-ču (mda' chu) the waters discharged from the lower parts of a valley, opp. to pú-ču (phu chu), those of the upper part Glr. — mda-ltóṅ (mda' ltong) the notch at that end of an arrow which is placed on the bowstring Pth. — mda-dár (mda' dar) a little flag fastened to an arrow; esp. an arrow with silk ribbons of five different colours. By hooking such an arrow into the collar of a bride, the match-maker draws her forth from among her maiden companions Glr. mda-dóṅ (mda' dong) quiver. — mdá-pa (mda' pa) an archer; mda-dpón (mda' dpon) the commander of the archers, a high military rank C. — mda-sprád (mda' sprad) v. spród-pa (sprod pa). — mda-bér (mda' ber) perh. the more correct form of ta-bér (ta ber). — mdá-bo (mda' bo) a large arrow. — mda-mó (mda' mo) arrow-lot, a kind of fortune-telling by means of arrows. — mda-rtséd byéd-pa (mda' rtsed byed pa) to amuse one's self with the shooting of arrows Cs. — mda-tśo (mda' tsho) a troop of archers Cs. — mda-gźu (mda' gzhu) bow and arrows Dzl. — mdá-bzo-pa (mda' bzo pa) arrow-maker. — mdá-yab (mda' yab) Glr. 1. Lex. = pú-śu (pu shu), fence; hence parapet, railing; yet a Lama from Tashilhunpo declared it to be the projecting part of the (flat) roofs of large temples, on which the parapet is erected. — 2. a covered gallery on the top of a house C.

1. lance, spear, pike, mduṅ-skór-ba (mdung skor ba) to brandish, to whirl a spear Cs.; mduṅ-ǩyim (mdung khyim) Dzl. 96, 9 a frame for leaning spears against; mduṅ-mǩan (mdung mkhan) a maker of spears; mduṅ-túṅ (mdung thung), or °táb-mduṅ ('thab mdung) a short lance or pike, a javelin. — mduṅ-togs (mdung thogs) Mil., mduṅ-pa (mdung pa) a spearsman, a lancer. — mduṅ-dár (mdung dar) a lance with a little flag at the top. — mduṅ-rtse (mdung rtse) top of a spear, spear-head; mduṅ-śiṅ (mdung shing) shaft of a lance. — mduṅ-bzo-pa (mdung bzo pa) =
